report.executive_summary: This contract contains 5 high-risk clause(s) and 1 medium-risk clause(s) that require attention before signing. Key issues include: The clause provides no measurable service level commitments , 7-day payment window is aggressive (vs standard Net-30) and , Liability cap of $500 is far below market standard of $50,00, Clause incorrectly assigns Vendor's pre-existing IP, tools, , 90-day notice period exceeds standard 30 days, creat.

missing_clauses.heading: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
missing_clauses.reason: GDPR Art. 28 mandates a DPA for any processing of EU personal data by a processor.

clause_table.heading: 1. SERVICES
clause_table.reason: The clause provides no measurable service level commitments (uptime percentages, response times) and uses vague "commercially reasonable efforts" standard with no remedies or credits. This leaves client with no recourse for poor service performance.
clause_table.original_text:
1. SERVICES
Vendor agrees to provide Client with access to the Acme SaaS Platform
("Platform") on the terms set out in this Agreement. Vendor will use
commercially reasonable efforts to maintain availability of the Platform.
clause_table.redline:
1. SERVICES
Vendor agrees to provide Client with access to the Acme SaaS Platform ("Platform") on the terms set out in this Agreement. Vendor warrants that the Platform will achieve a minimum Service Availability of 99.5% during each calendar month, measured as the percentage of total minutes in the month that the Platform is accessible and operational, excluding scheduled maintenance for which Vendor provides at least 24 hours' notice. If Service Availability falls below 99.5% in any month, Vendor will issue a service credit to Client equal to 10% of the monthly fees paid for that month for each full percentage point by which Service Availability is less than 99.5%. This service credit is Client's sole and exclusive remedy for any failure to meet the Service Availability commitment.

diff.text: Vendor agrees to provide Client with access to the Acme SaaS Platform
("Platform") on the terms set out in this Agreement. Vendor w

ill use
commercially reasonable efforts to maintain availability of the Platform

arrants that the Platform will achieve a minimum Service Availability of 99.5% during each calendar month, measured as the percentage of total minutes in the month that the Platform is accessible and operational, excluding scheduled maintenance for which Vendor provides at least 24 hours' notice. If Service Availability falls below 99.5% in any month, Vendor will issue a service credit to Client equal to 10% of the monthly fees paid for that month for each full percentage point by which Service Availability is less than 99.5%. This service credit is Client's sole and exclusive remedy for any failure to meet the Service Availability commitment

clause_table.redline_reason: Introduced a measurable uptime commitment with defined exclusions and a service credit remedy to replace the vague "commercially reasonable efforts" standard, providing the client with clear recourse for poor performance.

playbook_check.standard: 99.9% monthly uptime; service credits of 10% per 1% downtime below SLA
playbook_check.acceptable_range: 99.5% minimum uptime with any credit mechanism

clause_table.heading: 2. FEES AND PAYMENT
clause_table.reason: 7-day payment window is aggressive (vs standard Net-30) and 5% monthly interest (60% annual) is likely unlawful/unenforceable in many jurisdictions.
clause_table.original_text:
2. FEES AND PAYMENT
2.1 Client shall pay Vendor a monthly subscription fee of $5,000 per month.
2.2 Invoices are due and payable within 7 days of the invoice date. Late
payments shall accrue interest at 5% per month.
2.3 All fees are non-refundable.
clause_table.redline:
2. FEES AND PAYMENT
2.1 Client shall pay Vendor a monthly subscription fee of $5,000 per month.
2.2 Invoices are due and payable within 30 days of the invoice date. Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month.
2.3 All fees are non-refundable.

diff.text: 2.1 Client shall pay Vendor a monthly subscription fee of $5,000 per month.
2.2 Invoices are due and payable within
days of the invoice date. Late

clause_table.redline_reason: The payment window was changed from 7 days to 30 days to align with standard Net-30 terms, and the interest rate was reduced from 5% per month to 1.5% per month to ensure it is lawful and enforceable in most jurisdictions.

clause_table.heading: 3. TERM
clause_table.reason: Could not assess — defaulting to Low risk.
clause_table.original_text:
3. TERM
3.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an
initial term of 12 months.

clause_table.heading: 4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
clause_table.reason: Liability cap of $500 is far below market standard of $50,000 or 12-month fees; exposes client to unacceptable risk.
clause_table.original_text:
4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
4.1 In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, incidental, or
consequential damages.
4.2 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Vendor's total aggregate
liability to Client for any claims arising under or related to this
Agreement shall not exceed $500 (five hundred US dollars).
clause_table.redline:
4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

4.1 Neither party shall be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

4.2 Vendor’s total aggregate liability to Client for any claims arising under or related to this Agreement shall not exceed an amount equal to twelve (12) months of fees paid by Client to Vendor under this Agreement in the twelve (12) month period immediately preceding the claim giving rise to such liability; provided, however, that this limitation shall not apply to liability arising from Vendor’s fraud or willful misconduct.

diff.text: liability to Client for any claims arising under or related to this

an amount equal to twelve (12) months of fees paid by Client to Vendor under this Agreement in the twelve (12) month period immediately preceding the claim giving rise to such liability; provided, however, that this limitation shall not apply to liability arising from Vendor’s fraud or willful misconduct

clause_table.redline_reason: Increased Vendor's liability cap from $500 to twelve months of fees, a market-standard amount, and excluded fraud/willful misconduct to balance risk allocation fairly.

clause_table.heading: 5. CONFIDENTIALITY
clause_table.reason: Standard mutual confidentiality obligation with 3-year survival period, which aligns with market norms of 2-3 years.
clause_table.original_text:
5. CONFIDENTIALITY
5.1 Each party agrees to keep confidential all non-public information of
the other party disclosed in connection with this Agreement.
5.2 This confidentiality obligation shall survive for a period of 3 years
following termination of this Agreement.
5.3 Confidential information shall not include information that is publicly
available or independently developed.

clause_table.heading: 6. PAYMENT DISPUTES
clause_table.reason: 5-day dispute window is unreasonably short; waivers of disputes should allow at least 30 days for review.
clause_table.original_text:
6. PAYMENT DISPUTES
6.1 Client must raise any payment dispute in writing within 5 days of
receiving an invoice. Disputes not raised within this period are deemed
waived.
clause_table.redline:
6. PAYMENT DISPUTES
6.1 Client must raise any payment dispute in writing within thirty (30) days of receiving an invoice. Any amounts not disputed in writing within this period will be deemed accepted and payable, and Client waives the right to dispute such undisputed amounts.

diff.text: 6.1 Client must raise any payment dispute in writing within
Any amounts not disputed in writing
accepted and payable, and Client waives the right to dispute such undisputed amounts

clause_table.redline_reason: Extended the dispute window to a commercially reasonable period and clarified that the waiver applies only to amounts not timely disputed.

clause_table.heading: 7. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
clause_table.reason: Clause incorrectly assigns Vendor's pre-existing IP, tools, and methodologies to Client. Market standard: Vendor retains background IP; only custom work-for-hire transfers. This overly broad assignment is unacceptable.
clause_table.original_text:
7. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
7.1 Client acknowledges and agrees that all intellectual property rights in
and to the Platform, including all modifications, enhancements, and
derivative works, and all other work product created by Vendor in
connection with this Agreement, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive
property of Client. Vendor hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and
interest in and to such intellectual property, including all pre-existing
work, tools, and methodologies used by Vendor.
clause_table.redline_reason: Redline generation failed: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'strip'

playbook_check.standard: Vendor retains all pre-existing IP; only custom work-for-hire transfers
playbook_check.acceptable_range: Vendor grants broad licence for deliverables; full assignment only for bespoke work

clause_table.heading: 8. GOVERNING LAW
clause_table.reason: Delaware is a common, neutral jurisdiction but exclusive venue may impose travel/counsel costs on distant clients. Market standard.
clause_table.original_text:
8. GOVERNING LAW
8.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with
the laws of the State of Delaware. Any disputes shall be resolved
exclusively in the state or federal courts located in Delaware.

clause_table.heading: 9. TERMINATION
clause_table.reason: Standard mutual termination: 30-day convenience notice (market standard) and immediate right for material breach. Balanced and acceptable.
clause_table.original_text:
9. TERMINATION
9.1 Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon 30 days
written notice.
9.2 Either party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other
party materially breaches this Agreement.

playbook_check.standard: 30-day notice for convenience; 30-day cure period for breach

clause_table.heading: 10. GENERAL
clause_table.reason: Standard boilerplate: entire agreement and written amendment clauses. No unusual terms or risk.
clause_table.original_text:
10. GENERAL
10.1 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties
with respect to its subject matter.
10.2 No amendment to this Agreement shall be effective unless made in
writing and signed by both parties.

clause_table.heading: 11. RENEWAL
clause_table.reason: 90-day notice period exceeds standard 30 days, creating extended binding lock-in and high risk of accidental renewal.
clause_table.original_text:
11. RENEWAL
11.1 Upon expiration of the initial term, this Agreement will automatically
renew for successive 12-month periods.
11.3 Either party may prevent automatic renewal by providing written notice
to the other party at least 90 days prior to the end of the then-current
term. Failure to provide such notice within this window shall result in
automatic binding renewal.
clause_table.redline:
11. RENEWAL
11.1 Upon expiration of the initial term, this Agreement will automatically renew for successive 12-month periods.
11.3 Either party may prevent automatic renewal by providing written notice to the other party at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. If notice is not provided within this period, the Agreement will automatically renew.

11.3 Either party may prevent automatic renewal by providing written notice
to the other party at least
0 days prior to the end of the then-current
Failure to provide such notice within this window shall result in
If notice is not provided within this period, the Agreement will

clause_table.redline_reason: Reducing the notice period from 90 to 30 days aligns with market standards and mitigates the risk of an extended, unintended binding commitment.

playbook_check.standard: 30-day written notice to cancel before renewal

clause_table.heading: MISSING: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
clause_table.reason: GDPR Art. 28 mandates a DPA for any processing of EU personal data by a processor.
clause_table.redline_reason: GDPR Art. 28 mandates a DPA for any processing of EU personal data by a processor.

playbook_check.standard: A Data Processing Agreement must be in place before any EU personal data is processed.

action_checklist.action: Add SLA with 99.9% uptime commitment and service credits.
action_checklist.redline_reason: Introduced a measurable uptime commitment with defined exclusions and a service credit remedy to replace the vague "commercially reasonable efforts" standard, providing the client with clear recourse for poor performance.

action_checklist.action: Review payment terms and late fee structure against your cash flow requirements.
action_checklist.redline_reason: The payment window was changed from 7 days to 30 days to align with standard Net-30 terms, and the interest rate was reduced from 5% per month to 1.5% per month to ensure it is lawful and enforceable in most jurisdictions.

action_checklist.action: Redline liability cap to 12-month fees or $50,000 minimum before signing.
action_checklist.redline_reason: Increased Vendor's liability cap from $500 to twelve months of fees, a market-standard amount, and excluded fraud/willful misconduct to balance risk allocation fairly.
